summary: Joel shares a link to a VentureVoice interview with VC Brad Feld, who discusses the fundamental scaling problem with consulting businesses versus product businesses. Feld discovered that doubling headcount in a consulting firm doubles revenue but keeps profits flat. Joel highlights Feld's observation that transitioning from consulting to products is particularly difficult. The post also recommends a VentureVoice piece about launching companies at the DEMO Conference.
key_points: Consulting businesses have inherent scaling limitations: doubling headcount doubles revenue but not profit
The transition from consulting to product companies is notoriously difficult
Product businesses scale in ways that consulting cannot
money_quotes: We determined that the profit level we were generating at 20 people, well, if we grew to 40 people we'd obviously double the revenue, our profit would probably be exactly the same.
key_insight: Consulting businesses hit a profit ceiling because costs scale linearly with revenue, making products the superior model for building scalable, profitable companies.
